security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Malicious votes could alter the outcome of a proposal.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not validate the integrity of votes.

assumptions: The governance contract does not validate the contents of vote messages.
The actor is able to submit arbitrary data as a vote.

mitigation.strategy: Implement input validation to prevent malicious vote manipulation.

description: An attacker may submit a modified or malicious vote, potentially influencing the outcome of a proposal.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes are not immutably linked to users.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not provide robust audit trails for votes.

assumptions: The voting system does not log IP addresses or other identifying information.
The system does not require non-repudiable signatures.

mitigation.strategy: Implement a secure logging system and potentially require non-repudiable signatures.

description: An attacker may submit a vote and subsequently deny having done so.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes might be exposed due to insufficient access control.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system may expose vote information to unauthorized parties.

assumptions: The governance contract does not have sufficient access controls.
Vote information is stored in an insecure database or improperly sanitized.

mitigation.strategy: Implement appropriate access controls and data sanitization to protect vote information.

description: An attacker might be able to read vote information before it's processed or expose it through various means.
